seo.title: Advisory · Rosso Consulting
seo.description: Advisory portfolio of Rosso Consulting — four active roles in hospitality technology, expert network work, and independence framework.

hero.headline: Advisory
hero.sub: Four formal advisory roles in hospitality technology, plus expert network and investor work. The portfolio is curated; positions are taken only where the relationship offers strategic alignment with the wider practice.

roles.title: Current advisory roles
roles.intro: Active advisory positions, listed in order of joining.

lobbyai.fullDescription: AI-native hospitality startup. Marco joined the advisory board as the company closed its pre-seed financing. The engagement spans go-to-market design, sales and commercial strategy, and operator-side perspective on product direction.

canary-technologies.fullDescription: Established hospitality SaaS company. Marco serves as GTM Strategic Advisor focused on EU expansion and partnerships. The relationship continues a working history that began during Marco's tenure as Director of Sales and Go-To-Market EMEA at the company.

room-price-genie.fullDescription: Revenue management SaaS for independent hotels. Marco serves on the advisory board with focus on product direction and industry trends. The relationship contributes to Rosso Consulting's broader competitive intelligence in the revenue management category.

snapfix.fullDescription: Operations and facilities management SaaS for hotels and adjacent verticals. Marco serves on the advisory board with focus on product feedback and GTM perspective. Cadence of approximately 30 to 60 minutes per week.

expertNetwork.title: Expert network & investor work
expertNetwork.intro: Beyond formal advisory seats, Marco accepts paid consultations for venture capital, private equity, and corporate investors evaluating the hospitality technology sector. Engagement formats are flexible.

cards.label: Single calls
cards.p: 30 to 60 minute consultations booked through major expert networks. Used for due diligence on specific companies, sector orientation, or stress-testing a thesis.

cards.label: Project-based
cards.p: Multi-week engagements supporting investors through a specific transaction. Stack assessment, GTM evaluation, founder reference, and operator-side perspective on the target company.

cards.label: Retained
cards.p: Longer-term relationships with funds developing a thesis in the sector. Quarterly cadence with ad-hoc availability for fast-moving deals.

independence.title: Independence & disclosure
independence.intro: Holding both client engagements and advisory positions creates an obvious conflict surface. The conflicts are managed explicitly rather than left to circumstance.

items.label: Default
items.p: Where an advisory company's product may be relevant to a client engagement, the relationship is disclosed at the outset, before any vendor evaluation begins.

items.label: Practice
items.p: Client confidential information is never shared with advisory companies. Advisory company confidential information is never shared with clients. The barrier holds regardless of seeming overlap.

items.label: Vendor evaluation
items.p: When advisory companies are in a competitive evaluation set, they are scored against the same rubric as every other vendor, with the client retaining sole decision authority.

items.label: Limit
items.p: Either side can end the relationship if the conflict cannot be honestly managed. This has not yet been necessary; the option matters anyway.

cta.label: Need an advisor
cta.headline: Exploring an advisory seat
cta.sub: Founders building hospitality technology — pre-seed through Series B — can reach out about new advisory capacity. The bar is high; the reply is honest.
